title: Add `--version` flag to the CLI binary
body:
## Summary

The `soroforge` binary should print its version when invoked with `--version` or `-V`.

## Context

Clap's `derive` macro already supports this via `#[command(version)]`, which is present in `cli/src/main.rs`. However, the version is currently sourced from the workspace `Cargo.toml`. This issue tracks verifying the flag works end-to-end and adding a smoke test.

## Implementation Steps

1. Run `cargo run -- --version` and confirm it prints `soroforge 0.1.0`.
2. Add a CLI integration test in `tests/integration/` that spawns the binary and asserts the output matches the semver pattern `^soroforge \d+\.\d+\.\d+$`.
3. Ensure the test is included in `cargo test --all`.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `soroforge --version` prints the correct version string.
- [ ] An integration test asserts the version output format.
- [ ] CI passes with the new test.

title: Implement `soroforge init` â€” full project scaffold
body:
## Summary

The `soroforge init <name>` command currently creates a minimal directory and a stub `soroforge.toml`. It should scaffold a complete, ready-to-build Soroban project.

## Desired Output Structure

```
<name>/
â”śâ”€â”€ contracts/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ hello_world/
â”‚       â”śâ”€â”€ Cargo.toml
â”‚       â””â”€â”€ src/lib.rs        # minimal #[contract] stub
â”śâ”€â”€ soroforge.toml
â””â”€â”€ .gitignore
```

## Implementation Steps

1. In `cli/src/commands/init.rs`, extend `run()` to write each file above using embedded template strings.
2. The `contracts/hello_world/src/lib.rs` template should contain a minimal `#[contract]` struct with a single `hello(env: Env) -> String` method.
3. The generated `Cargo.toml` for the contract should target `cdylib` and depend on `soroban-sdk`.
4. Print a success message listing the created files.
5. Return an error (not a panic) if the target directory already exists.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Running `soroforge init my_project` creates the full structure above.
- [ ] Re-running on an existing directory returns a clear error.
- [ ] Unit tests cover both the happy path and the duplicate-directory error.
- [ ] CI passes.

title: Implement `soroforge sandbox start/stop` via Docker
body:
## Summary

The `soroforge sandbox` command should start and stop a local Soroban node using the `stellar/quickstart` Docker image.

## Background

The stub in `crates/soroforge-sandbox/src/sandbox.rs` has `start()` and `stop()` methods with `// TODO` comments. This issue implements them.

## Implementation Steps

1. Add `bollard = "0.17"` to `crates/soroforge-sandbox/Cargo.toml` for async Docker API access.
2. In `Sandbox::start()`:
   - Pull `stellar/quickstart:latest` if not present.
   - Create and start a container with the `--standalone` flag, binding the RPC port to `self.port`.
   - Write the container ID to `.soroforge/sandbox.pid` in the current working directory.
   - Poll the RPC endpoint (`/health`) until it responds 200 or a 60-second timeout elapses.
3. In `Sandbox::stop()`:
   - Read the container ID from `.soroforge/sandbox.pid`.
   - Stop and remove the container.
   - Delete the `.pid` file.
4. Add `--stop` flag handling in `cli/src/commands/sandbox.rs` (already present in the stub).

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `soroforge sandbox` starts a local node and prints the RPC URL.
- [ ] `soroforge sandbox --stop` cleanly shuts it down.
- [ ] A Docker-gated integration test in `tests/integration/` verifies start/stop.
- [ ] CI passes (Docker tests can be skipped with `#[ignore]` if Docker is unavailable).

title: Implement `soroforge deploy` â€” WASM upload and contract instantiation
body:
## Summary

The `soroforge deploy` command should upload a compiled `.wasm` file to the Stellar network and instantiate the contract, printing the resulting contract ID.

## Background

`crates/soroforge-deploy/src/deployer.rs` has a `deploy()` stub. This issue replaces the placeholder with a real implementation using the `stellar-xdr` and `stellar-sdk` crates (or shell-out to `stellar contract deploy`).

## Implementation Steps

1. Decide on approach: shell-out to `stellar contract deploy` (simpler) vs. native Rust XDR (more control). Document the decision in `docs/adr-001-deploy-strategy.md`.
2. If shelling out:
   - Use `tokio::process::Command` to invoke `stellar contract deploy --wasm <path> --network <name> --source <key>`.
   - Parse the contract ID from stdout.
3. Add `--source-account` and `--secret-key` (or `--secret-key-env`) flags to `DeployArgs` in `cli/src/commands/deploy.rs`.
4. Load network config from `soroforge.toml` if `--network` matches a named entry.
5. Print the contract ID on success; print a structured error on failure.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `soroforge deploy --wasm <file> --name <name> --network testnet` returns a real contract ID.
- [ ] Secret key is never logged or printed.
- [ ] An integration test deploys the `hello_world` contract from `soroforge init` to a local sandbox.
- [ ] CI passes.

title: Implement `soroforge watch` â€” hot-reload on contract file changes
body:
## Summary

The `soroforge watch` command should watch a contracts directory for `.rs` file changes, trigger `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`, and redeploy to the running sandbox automatically.

## Background

The Rust stub in `cli/src/commands/watch.rs` prints a placeholder message. The TypeScript `@soroforge/hot-reload` package (`packages/hot-reload/src/watcher.ts`) provides a reference implementation using `chokidar`. This issue implements the Rust-native version.

## Implementation Steps

1. Add `notify = { version = "6.1", features = ["macos_fsevent"] }` to `cli/Cargo.toml`.
2. In `commands/watch.rs`, set up a `notify::RecommendedWatcher` on `args.dir`.
3. On each `EventKind::Modify` event for a `.rs` file:
   a. Debounce events within a 300 ms window to avoid duplicate builds.
   b. Run `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --release` in the contract's directory.
   c. On build success, call `soroforge_deploy::Deployer::deploy()` against the local sandbox.
   d. Print a timestamped status line: `[HH:MM:SS] âś… Rebuilt & redeployed <contract>`.
4. Handle `Ctrl-C` (SIGINT) gracefully â€” stop the watcher and print a goodbye message.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Saving a `.rs` file triggers a rebuild and redeploy within ~2 seconds.
- [ ] Debouncing prevents duplicate builds on rapid saves.
- [ ] `Ctrl-C` exits cleanly.
- [ ] A unit test verifies the debounce logic.
- [ ] CI passes.

title: Load project config from `soroforge.toml`
body:
## Summary

All subcommands should automatically load `soroforge.toml` from the current working directory (or a parent directory, walking up the tree) and use its values as defaults, with CLI flags taking precedence.

## Implementation Steps

1. In `crates/soroforge-core/src/config.rs`, add a `SoroForgeConfig::load() -> Result<Self>` function that:
   - Walks up from `std::env::current_dir()` looking for `soroforge.toml`.
   - Parses the file with `toml::from_str` (add `toml = "0.8"` to workspace deps).
   - Returns `SoroForgeConfig::default()` if no file is found.
2. Call `SoroForgeConfig::load()` at the start of each command's `run()` function.
3. Merge CLI flag values over config file values (CLI wins).
4. Add a `--config <path>` global flag to `Cli` in `cli/src/main.rs` for explicit config file override.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `soroforge deploy` reads `network.rpc_url` from `soroforge.toml` when `--network` is not passed.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: file found, file not found (uses defaults), malformed TOML (returns error).
- [ ] CI passes.

title: Publish `@soroforge/sdk` â€” typed TypeScript client
body:
## Summary

The `packages/sdk` package contains a skeleton `SoroForgeClient`. This issue fleshes it out into a usable TypeScript SDK that wraps `@stellar/stellar-sdk` for common Soroban operations.

## Implementation Steps

1. Add a `ContractClient` class in `packages/sdk/src/contract.ts` with:
   - `call(method: string, args: xdr.ScVal[]): Promise<xdr.ScVal>` â€” simulate a contract call.
   - `invoke(method: string, args: xdr.ScVal[], signer: Keypair): Promise<string>` â€” submit a transaction and return the txHash.
2. Export `ContractClient` from `packages/sdk/src/index.ts`.
3. Add Jest unit tests in `packages/sdk/src/__tests__/` that mock `@stellar/stellar-sdk` network calls.
4. Add a `tsconfig.build.json` that excludes test files from the published output.
5. Add a `prepublishOnly` script: `npm run build && npm test`.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `ContractClient.call()` and `ContractClient.invoke()` are implemented and tested.
- [ ] `npm run build` produces valid `.d.ts` files.
- [ ] All Jest tests pass.
- [ ] CI passes.

title: Add structured error output and `--json` flag
body:
## Summary

All CLI commands should support a `--json` flag that emits machine-readable JSON output instead of human-readable text. This enables scripting and IDE integrations.

## Implementation Steps

1. Add a global `--json` flag to `Cli` in `cli/src/main.rs`.
2. Pass a `json: bool` value down to each command's `run()` function.
3. Define a generic `Output<T: Serialize>` enum:
   ```rust
   enum Output<T> { Success(T), Error { message: String, code: u32 } }
   ```
4. Each command prints either `println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&output)?)` (JSON mode) or a human-readable string (default mode).
5. On error, exit with a non-zero status code in both modes.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `soroforge deploy --json` emits `{"contractId": "..."}` on success.
- [ ] `soroforge deploy --json` emits `{"error": "...", "code": 1}` on failure.
- [ ] Unit tests cover both output modes for at least one command.
- [ ] CI passes.

title: Write architecture documentation and ADR template
body:
## Summary

The `docs/` directory is empty. This issue populates it with an architecture overview and an ADR (Architecture Decision Record) template so contributors understand design decisions.

## Files to Create

### `docs/architecture.md`

Should cover:
- High-level component diagram (ASCII or Mermaid) showing `cli` â†’ `soroforge-deploy` â†’ Stellar RPC.
- Data flow for `soroforge deploy`: CLI args â†’ config merge â†’ WASM upload â†’ instantiate â†’ print contract ID.
- Data flow for `soroforge watch`: file event â†’ debounce â†’ build â†’ deploy.
- How the TypeScript packages relate to the Rust CLI.

### `docs/adr-000-template.md`

Standard ADR template with sections: **Status**, **Context**, **Decision**, **Consequences**.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `docs/architecture.md` contains a Mermaid diagram and prose descriptions of all major data flows.
- [ ] `docs/adr-000-template.md` exists and follows the standard ADR format.
- [ ] Links to both files are added to `README.md` under a new `## Documentation` section.
- [ ] CI passes (no code changes required).

title: Add end-to-end integration test: init â†’ sandbox â†’ deploy
body:
## Summary

Create a full end-to-end integration test in `tests/integration/` that exercises the complete developer workflow: scaffold a project, start a local sandbox, deploy the generated contract, and assert the contract ID is returned.

## Prerequisites

- Issues #3 (sandbox) and #4 (deploy) must be merged first.
- Docker must be available in the test environment.

## Implementation Steps

1. Create `tests/integration/e2e_workflow.rs`.
2. The test should:
   a. Call `soroforge init test_project` in a `tempdir`.
   b. Start a sandbox on port `8001` (to avoid conflicts).
   c. Build the generated `hello_world` contract to WASM.
   d. Call `soroforge deploy` against the sandbox.
   e. Assert the returned contract ID matches the Stellar contract ID format (`C[A-Z2-7]{55}`).
   f. Stop the sandbox.
3. Gate the test with `#[ignore]` so it only runs when `cargo test -- --include-ignored` is called (or in CI with Docker available).
4. Add a CI job step that runs `cargo test --test e2e_workflow -- --include-ignored` with `services: docker`.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] The integration test passes locally with Docker.
- [ ] The test is gated with `#[ignore]` and documented.
- [ ] CI has a dedicated job that runs the test with Docker available.
- [ ] CI passes.
